reasoning: Despite the high political motivation to prosecute James Comey under the current administration (including an active FBI probe launched by Kash Patel in February 2025 and public demands from figures like Tulsi Gabbard and Senator Tom Cotton), the timeframe for an arrest before July 1, 2025, is very tight (only 1 month remaining from the forecast date of May 30). The recent controversy regarding his "86 47" social media post resulted in a voluntary interview with the Secret Service where he was not taken into custody, suggesting that an arrest on "true threat" charges is unlikely given First Amendment protections. Consequently, any arrest would likely need to stem from a larger FBI/DOJ investigation, which typically takes longer than the remaining 30 days to result in an indictment and arrest.
